title: Audio Not Working
cardText: Diagnose computers with no sound, low sound, or missing audio devices.
Check volume levels, output devices, headphones, speakers, Bluetooth connections, audio services, and drivers.
Separate software configuration issues from hardware faults.
text: This guide is for computers where audio is missing, distorted, or not working correctly.
Start with simple checks such as volume and output device selection.
Then investigate applications, drivers, services, and hardware.
Escalate hardware faults after software troubleshooting is completed.

checkMute.title: Check mute status
body: Verify the computer is not muted.
Check the keyboard mute key, volume icon, and any external speaker controls.
Muting is one of the most common causes of missing audio.

unmuteAudio.title: Unmute audio
body: The system was muted.
Unmute audio and test sound playback again.

checkVolumeLevel.title: Check volume level
body: Ensure the volume is turned up to an audible level.
Check both the master volume and application volume controls.
Very low volume can appear as no sound.

increaseVolume.title: Increase volume
body: Raise the volume level and test audio again.
Check application-specific volume controls as well.

testAudioPlayback.title: Test audio playback
body: Play a known audio file or video.
Confirm whether any sound is heard.
This helps verify whether the issue affects all audio.

appSpecificIssue.title: Application-specific audio issue
body: Audio works in some applications but not others.
Check the application's audio settings, output device, and volume controls.
The operating system audio system appears to be functioning.

checkOutputDevice.title: Check audio output device
body: Open sound settings and review the selected output device.
Computers can route audio to monitors, Bluetooth devices, USB headsets, or speakers.
Selecting the wrong device results in no audible sound.

selectCorrectDevice.title: Select correct audio device
body: The wrong output device was selected.
Choose the correct speakers, headset, or monitor and test again.

checkHeadphonesSpeakers.title: Check headphones or speakers
body: Verify speakers are powered on if required.
Reconnect headphones or speakers.
Check volume controls on external devices.

repairAudioDevice.title: Repair or replace audio device
body: The headphones or speakers appear faulty.
Repair, reconnect, or replace the affected device.

checkBluetoothAudio.title: Check Bluetooth audio devices
body: Determine whether audio is being sent to a Bluetooth device.
Bluetooth devices can remain connected even when not being used.
Disconnect unused Bluetooth audio devices and test again.

disconnectBluetooth.title: Disconnect Bluetooth audio device
body: Audio was being routed to a Bluetooth device.
Disconnect or reconfigure the device and test audio again.

checkAudioServices.title: Check audio services
body: Verify Windows Audio services are running.
Audio services are responsible for sound playback and device management.
Restarting these services can resolve some audio faults.

restartAudioServices.title: Restart audio services
body: Restart the Windows Audio services.
Retest audio playback after the services restart.

checkAudioDeviceManager.title: Check Device Manager
body: Open Device Manager and inspect audio devices.
Look for warning icons, missing devices, or disabled hardware.
Driver problems often appear here.

updateAudioDriver.title: Update or reinstall audio driver
body: Update the audio driver from the manufacturer if available.
Alternatively remove and reinstall the device.
Restart the computer after driver changes.

testAlternateAudioDevice.title: Test another audio device
body: Connect another headset, speaker, or monitor with audio.
This helps separate computer faults from speaker or headphone faults.
Retest audio playback.

audioDeviceFault.title: Audio device fault found
body: Audio works using another device.
The original speaker, headset, or monitor audio hardware is likely faulty.

checkSystemUpdates.title: Check recent updates
body: Determine whether the issue started after a Windows or driver update.
Recent updates can sometimes introduce audio problems.
Review update history if applicable.

rollbackDriver.title: Rollback audio driver
body: The issue appears related to a recent update.
Rollback the audio driver or restore a previous working version if appropriate.
Retest audio afterwards.

hardwareEscalation.title: Further hardware diagnostics required
body: Volume, devices, drivers, services, and updates have been checked.
Possible causes include failed audio hardware, motherboard faults, damaged ports, or internal speaker failure.
Escalate for hardware diagnostics.
